sourceMovie.title: The Great Gatsby
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Great Gatsby works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Revolutionary Road
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Great Gatsby clicked for you, Revolutionary Road (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Choice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Great Gatsby clicked for you, The Choice (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Great Gatsby?
faq.answer: Try Revolutionary Road, The Choice, If Beale Street Could Talk—each shares DNA with The Great Gatsby while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Great Gatsby?
faq.answer: The Great Gatsby (2013) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Great Gatsby worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
